title: Project — IELTS Speaking
topics: IELTS speaking parts · fluency strategies · topic expansion · answers
description: Projekt: příprava na IELTS Speaking — strategie, rozšiřování odpovědí, Part 1/2/3.
notes: Part 1: Short answers — 2–3 sentences. Don't just say 'yes' or 'no'.
Part 2: Long turn — speak for 1–2 minutes on a card topic. Plan for 1 min.
Part 3: Discussion — abstract questions. Give opinions with reasons + examples.
✓ Buying time: 'That's an interesting question...', 'Let me think about that...'
✓ Expanding: 'Not only that, but...', 'What's more...', 'To give you an example...'
✓ Showing fluency: vary vocabulary, use connectors, avoid repetition
⚠️  Avoid: very long pauses, answering in one sentence, repeating the question

examples.en: Q: Do you like cooking? A: Yes, I really enjoy it. I cook most evenings and I particularly love experimenting with new recipes. Recently I've been trying to learn more Italian dishes.

Q: Should governments invest more in public transport? A: Absolutely. Not only does it reduce traffic congestion, but it also helps lower carbon emissions. Countries like Japan show that high-quality public transport is completely feasible.
To give you an example of what I mean...
examples.cz: Abych vám dal/a příklad toho, co myslím...

examples.en: That's an interesting question — let me think about that for a moment.
examples.cz: To je zajímavá otázka — nechte mě chvíli přemýšlet.

quiz.sentence: Not only does it save time, ___ it also reduces costs. (but)
To ___ you an example — in Sweden, renewable energy covers 80% of demand.
That's a great question. Let me ___ about that.
What's ___, it's also better for the environment.

tasks: 1. Record yourself answering 5 IELTS Part 1 questions for 1–2 mins each. Listen back.
2. Write a 2-minute Part 2 answer: 'Describe a book that influenced you.'
3. Answer this Part 3 question in 150 words: 'How will technology change education in the next 20 years?'
